entries.body: Any admissible specification declares a finite registry of atomic tokens used as carriers for all subsequent relations.

entries.label: NONCOMMUTATION
entries.body: Admissible composition is not globally commutative; at least one admissible specification contains an order-sensitivity witness as a relation-instance.

entries.label: NO_PRIMITIVE_IDENTITY
entries.body: No admissible specification may designate a privileged token as an identity element for all admissible compositions.

entries.label: NO_PRIMITIVE_EQUALITY
entries.body: No admissible specification may introduce a primitive substitutive equality predicate on tokens.

entries.label: ADMISSIBLE_SPEC_SCHEMA
entries.body: An admissible specification consists of (i) a finite token registry, (ii) a finite set of relation symbols with declared arities, and (iii) finite relation-instances interpreting those symbols over th

entries.label: COMPOSITION_RELATION
entries.body: If a composition notion is admitted, it is represented as a ternary relation Comp(x,y,z) over tokens, with no totality assumed.

entries.label: DISTINGUISHABILITY_RELATION
entries.body: If a distinction notion is admitted, it is represented as a binary relation Dist(x,y) over tokens, with no symmetry assumed.

entries.label: ORDER_SENSITIVITY_WITNESS
entries.body: Order sensitivity is admitted only via finite witnesses of the form Comp(a,b,u), Comp(b,a,v), Dist(u,v), where a,b,u,v are tokens.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_COMMUTATION_AXIOM
entries.body: No admissible specification may include a universal commutation axiom schema that forces order insensitivity for all composable pairs.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible specification may include a universal substitution schema that permits replacing a token by another token across all relation-instances.

entries.label: NO_CANONICAL_IDENTITY_LAW
entries.body: No admissible specification may include a universal identity-law schema asserting existence of a token e satisfying Comp(e,x,x) and Comp(x,e,x) for all tokens x.

entries.label: PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: Totality of Comp is not assumed; any closure or total-composability condition must be explicitly declared and is not implied by admissibility.

entries.label: ASSOCIATIVITY_NOT_ASSUMED
entries.body: Associativity of composition is not assumed; any associativity claim must be explicitly declared and is not implied by admissibility.

entries.label: INVERTIBILITY_NOT_ASSUMED
entries.body: Invertibility of composition is not assumed; any inverse-like claim must be explicitly declared and is not implied by admissibility.

entries.label: CONGRUENCE_NOT_ASSUMED
entries.body: Compatibility of any equivalence-like relation with composition is not assumed; any congruence-like claim must be explicitly declared and is not implied by admissibility.

entries.label: DERIVED_EQUIVALENCE
entries.body: A derived equivalence-like relation may be introduced only as a definition from explicitly declared finite relation-instances; no global substitution power is implied.

entries.label: COMMUTATIVE_SUBCLASS
entries.body: Admissibility of explicitly declared commutative sub-specifications is open, provided no global commutation axiom is imposed on the overall admissible class.

entries.label: IDENTITY_SURROGATE
entries.body: Admissibility of identity-surrogate notions defined purely from declared relations is open, provided no universal identity law or substitution schema is introduced.

entries.label: MINIMALITY_CRITERIA
entries.body: Criteria for minimal admissible relation vocabularies sufficient to witness noncommutation without primitive equality are open.

entries.label: FINITUDE
entries.body: Any admissible transport specification is given by a finite description over a finite alphabet.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: Any admissible transport specification presupposes a frozen state abstraction layer and ranges only over declared state tokens.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_SYMBOL_ADMISSIBILITY
entries.body: A transport notion is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Tr(s,t) over the finite state-token registry.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_WELLTYPEDNESS
entries.body: Any asserted Tr(s,t) is admissible only if both s and t are declared state tokens in the state-token registry.

entries.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_TRANSPORT
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may assert that Tr(s,t) implies identity or equality of s and t.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_TRANSPORT
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may introduce any schema permitting replacement of s by t (or t by s) in arbitrary relations on the basis of Tr(s,t).

entries.label: TRANSPORT_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may assume Tr(s,t) holds for all ordered pairs of state tokens.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_NONREFLEXIVE_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may assume Tr(s,s) holds for all state tokens s.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_NONSYMMETRIC_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may assume Tr(s,t) implies Tr(t,s).

entries.label: TRANSPORT_NONTRANSITIVE_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may assume Tr(s,t) and Tr(t,u) imply Tr(s,u).

entries.label: TRANSPORT_COMPOSITION_SYMBOL
entries.body: If a composition of transport is admitted, it is admissible only as an explicitly declared ternary relation symbol TrC(s,t,u) with no closure, associativity, commutativity, identity, or invertibility

entries.label: ORDER_SENSITIVITY_WITNESS
entries.body: Any claim that transport composition is order-sensitive is admissible only via finite witnesses of the form TrC(a,b,u), TrC(b,a,v), DistS(u,v), where DistS is an admissible distinguishability relation

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_COMMUTATION_OF_TRANSPORT
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may include a universal axiom schema forcing TrC(a,b,·) to be indistinguishable from TrC(b,a,·) for all a,b.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_WITNESSING_BINDING
entries.body: Any assertion of Tr(s,t) is admissible only if accompanied by a finite witness binding WTr(s,t,w), where w is a token serving solely as a syntactic witness carrier.

entries.label: WITNESS_NONSEMANTIC
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may treat witness tokens as semantic evidence, valuation, score, or truth-bearers; WTr binds only syntactic admissibility support.

entries.label: WITNESS_SUPPORT_REQUIREMENT
entries.body: Any admissible WTr(s,t,w) must be supported by finite declared bindings Abs(s,Σs), Abs(t,Σt) and declared representatives Rep(s,x), Rep(t,y) such that a declared relation instance R(x,y) exists in at

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_TRIVIALIZATION
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may assert that transport trivializes globally by forcing Tr(s,t) for all state-token pairs.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_IDENTITY_TRANSPORT
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may assert the existence of a universal transport identity law making Tr(s,s) hold for all s.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CANONICAL_TRANSPORT
entries.body: No admissible transport specification may assert uniqueness or canonicity of transport between any ordered pair (s,t) without explicit finite witness declarations.

entries.label: INVERTIBLE_TRANSPORT
entries.body: Admissibility of an explicitly declared inverse-like relation InvTr(t,s) associated to Tr(s,t) is open, provided no substitution schema or identity/equality is introduced.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_EQUIVALENCE_CLASSES
entries.body: Admissibility of derived equivalence-like quotients induced by transport relations is open, provided no primitive equality or universal substitution is introduced.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_LOOP_EFFECTS
entries.body: Admissibility of explicitly declared loop-composition effects (nontrivial composites of transport around declared cycles) is open, provided no geometric, metric, temporal, or motion semantics are intr

entries.label: FINITUDE
entries.body: Any admissible obstruction specification is given by a finite description over a finite alphabet.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: Any admissible obstruction specification presupposes frozen state abstraction and transport layers and ranges only over declared transport-related relation instances.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_SYMBOL_ADMISSIBILITY
entries.body: An obstruction notion is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Obs(τ,κ), where τ and κ are transport-related relation instances over state tokens.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_WELLTYPEDNESS
entries.body: Any asserted Obs(τ,κ) is admissible only if τ and κ are explicitly declared transport-related relation instances formed from symbols admitted in the transport layer.

entries.label: NONTRIVIALIZATION_FAILURE_FORM
entries.body: A failure of transport trivialization is admissible only as a finite witnessed claim that two transport-related composites are distinguishable by the frozen state distinguishability relation.

entries.label: COMPOSITE_TRANSPORT_INSTANCE
entries.body: If transport composition is used in an obstruction claim, it must be represented only via explicitly declared instances of TrC(·,·,·) over state tokens; no implicit composite formation is admissible.

entries.label: FAILURE_WITNESS_PATTERN
entries.body: Any admissible obstruction witness must have the finite pattern TrC(a,b,u), TrC(c,d,v), DistS(u,v), together with admissible transport-witness bindings supporting the involved transport assertions; no

entries.label: NO_GEOMETRIC_READING
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may introduce any geometric, metric, coordinate, neighborhood, or embedding interpretation for Obs or its witnesses.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_WITNESSING_BINDING
entries.body: Any assertion of Obs(τ,κ) is admissible only if accompanied by a finite witness binding WObs(τ,κ,w), where w is a token serving solely as a syntactic witness carrier.

entries.label: WITNESS_NONSEMANTIC
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may treat witness tokens as semantic evidence, valuation, score, or truth-bearer; WObs binds only syntactic admissibility support.

entries.label: NONIDENTITY_FROM_OBSTRUCTION
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assert that Obs(τ,κ) implies identity or equality of any involved state tokens or transport instances.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_OBSTRUCTION
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any state token or transport instance on the basis of Obs(τ,κ).

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assume Obs(τ,κ) holds for all transport-related instance pairs.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_NONREFLEXIVE_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assume Obs(τ,τ) holds for all transport-related instances τ.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_NONSYMMETRIC_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assume Obs(τ,κ) implies Obs(κ,τ).

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_NONTRANSITIVE_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assume Obs(τ,κ) and Obs(κ,λ) imply Obs(τ,λ).

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_FLATNESS_AXIOM
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may include a universal axiom schema forcing absence of obstruction for all transport-related instance pairs.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_OBSTRUCTION_AXIOM
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may include a universal axiom schema forcing obstruction for all transport-related instance pairs.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CANONICAL_OBSTRUCTION
entries.body: No admissible obstruction specification may assert uniqueness or canonicity of obstruction witnesses beyond explicitly declared finite witnesses.

entries.label: CURVATURE_LIKE_RELATIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of explicitly declared higher-arity relations aggregating multiple obstruction instances is open, provided no geometric, metric, temporal, or motion semantics are introduced.

entries.label: HOLONOMY_CLASS_TOKENS
entries.body: Admissibility of class tokens representing families of obstruction-witness patterns is open, provided no primitive equality or substitution schema is introduced.

entries.label: TRIVIALIZATION_SCHEMES
entries.body: Admissibility of explicitly declared local trivialization schemes (restricted collections of transport instances where obstruction is absent) is open, provided no geometry, neighborhood, or metric lan

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This derivation presupposes frozen admissibility of state tokens, transport relations, and obstruction relations.

entries.label: GLOBAL_CARRIER_SYMBOL
entries.body: A minimal global carrier is admissible only as an explicitly declared token registry M whose elements are carrier tokens m.

entries.label: STATE_INDEXING_MAP
entries.body: Any admissible global carrier M must admit an explicitly declared indexing relation Ind(s,m) binding each declared state token s to at least one carrier token m in M; no uniqueness is implied.

entries.label: NO_CANONICAL_POINTS
entries.body: No admissible derivation may assume a canonical distinguished carrier token in M.

entries.label: COMPATIBILITY_RELATION
entries.body: Compatibility induced by transport is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation CompM(m,n) over carrier tokens, supported by the existence of at least one admissible transport instance

entries.label: NO_TOTAL_COMPATIBILITY
entries.body: No admissible derivation may assume CompM(m,n) holds for all carrier token pairs (m,n).

entries.label: NO_CONNECTEDNESS_ASSUMPTION
entries.body: No admissible derivation may assume that the carrier tokens of M form a single compatibility component under CompM.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_LIFTING_RELATION
entries.body: Obstruction is admissible on the carrier only via an explicitly declared relation ObsM((m,n),(p,q)) whose instances are supported by underlying obstruction instances Obs(τ,κ) together with compatibili

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_INDUCED_NONTRIVIALITY
entries.body: If there exists any admissible obstruction instance Obs(τ,κ), then admissibility requires that the global carrier structure include at least one nontrivial obstruction-lift instance ObsM((m,n),(p,q))

entries.label: PATH_TOKEN_ADMISSIBILITY
entries.body: Path organization is admissible only via explicitly declared finite path tokens π and an endpoint relation End(π,m,n) over carrier tokens.

entries.label: PATH_SUPPORT_BY_TRANSPORT
entries.body: Any asserted End(π,m,n) is admissible only if supported by a finite chain of admissible transport instances Tr(s0,s1),…,Tr(sk-1,sk) together with indexing Ind(si,mi) such that m0=m and mk=n are carrie

entries.label: PATH_CONCATENATION_SYMBOL
entries.body: If a concatenation notion is admitted on paths, it is admissible only as an explicitly declared ternary relation Cat(π1,π2,π3) with no associativity, identity, or totality implied.

entries.label: NONFLATTENABILITY_CONDITION
entries.body: Global trivialization of transport is forbidden whenever there exists a witnessed obstruction instance such that two path tokens with the same declared endpoints are supported by transport chains whos

entries.label: NO_UNIVERSAL_TRIVIALIZATION_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible derivation may include a universal schema asserting that for all carrier tokens m,n there exists a path token π with End(π,m,n) supported by transport and producing indistinguishable com

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_METRIC_STRUCTURE
entries.body: No admissible derivation may introduce any numeric or ordered distance-like relation on carrier tokens.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_COORDINATE_STRUCTURE
entries.body: No admissible derivation may introduce any coordinate assignment, chart, or tuple-valued labeling of carrier tokens that carries substitution power.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_DIMENSION_ASSERTION
entries.body: No admissible derivation may assert any dimensionality property of M.

entries.label: GEOMETRY_AS_EXTENSION
entries.body: Admissibility of introducing geometry-like structure on M is open, provided it is derived from CompM, path organization, and obstruction-lift relations and does not assume metric primitives.

entries.label: CURVATURE_AS_EXTENSION
entries.body: Admissibility of curvature-like invariants defined purely from obstruction-lift behavior and transport composition is open.

entries.label: DIMENSIONALITY_AS_EXTENSION
entries.body: Admissibility of derived dimensionality notions from purely relational properties of M is open, provided no coordinate or metric assumptions are introduced.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes a frozen global carrier M with compatibility CompM, transport Tr/TrC, and obstruction Obs/ObsM as previously admitted.

entries.label: GEOMETRIC_STRUCTURE_SCHEMA
entries.body: A geometric structure on M is admissible only as a finite collection of explicitly declared relation symbols over carrier tokens and path tokens, together with finite relation-instances, and with no a

entries.label: INCIDENCE_RELATION_ADMISSIBILITY
entries.body: An incidence notion is admissible only as an explicitly declared relation Inc(m,π,n) supported by End(π,m,n) and compatibility CompM(m,n).

entries.label: LOCALITY_RELATION_ADMISSIBILITY
entries.body: A locality notion is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation Loc(m,n) whose instances are supported by CompM(m,n); no symmetry, reflexivity, or transitivity is implied.

entries.label: NO_METRIC_PRIMITIVES
entries.body: No admissible geometric structure may introduce any numeric-valued or ordered distance-like, norm-like, or length-like primitive on carrier tokens or paths.

entries.label: NO_METRIC_DERIVATION_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible geometric structure may include a universal schema deriving a distance-like ordering from compatibility, transport, or obstruction.

entries.label: NO_COORDINATE_ASSIGNMENT
entries.body: No admissible geometric structure may introduce any coordinate assignment, chart, tuple-labeling, or coordinate-valued function on carrier tokens.

entries.label: NO_DIMENSION_ASSERTION
entries.body: No admissible geometric structure may assert any dimensionality property of M or any coordinate-rank property.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_COMPATIBLE_GEOMETRY
entries.body: Any admissible geometric relation that references paths must be supported by declared transport chains witnessing End(π,m,n); no independent path semantics is admissible.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_COMPOSITION_COMPATIBILITY
entries.body: Any admissible geometric relation that references concatenation must be supported by explicitly declared Cat(π1,π2,π3) instances and must not assume associativity or identity of concatenation.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_RESPECT
entries.body: No admissible geometric structure may include any schema forcing all transport-supported path composites between the same endpoints to be indistinguishable whenever a witnessed obstruction instance ex

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_FLAT_GEOMETRY_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible geometric structure may include a universal schema asserting global flattenability of transport-supported path composites across all compatible endpoints.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_EMBEDDING_ASSERTION
entries.body: No admissible geometric structure may assert existence of an embedding of M into any coordinate-bearing or metric-bearing ambient structure.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CONTINUITY_ASSUMPTION
entries.body: No admissible geometric structure may assume continuity, limits, convergence, or openness properties of any relation on M.

entries.label: METRIC_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of metric-like structures is open, provided they are derived without introducing metric primitives and do not trivialize obstruction.

entries.label: DIMENSION_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of derived dimensionality notions is open, provided no coordinate or rank primitives are introduced.

entries.label: COORDINATE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of coordinate-like representations is open, provided they are treated as removable overlays and introduce no substitution power.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes a frozen carrier M, compatibility CompM, path tokens π with End, transport Tr/TrC, and obstruction Obs/ObsM, and a frozen geometry layer.

entries.label: METRIC_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: A metric-like structure is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Met(m,n,v) or MetP(π,σ,v) where v is a token from an explicitly declared finite value-token registry V.

entries.label: VALUE_TOKEN_NONNUMERIC
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may treat value tokens v as numbers, ordered quantities, or elements of a completed infinite domain.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_METRIC_TOTALITY
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may assume Met(m,n,·) is defined for all carrier token pairs (m,n), nor that for any pair there exists any v.

entries.label: NO_CANONICAL_VALUE
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may assume a canonical distinguished value token (e.g., a “zero” or “unit” value) across all Met instances.

entries.label: NONTRIVIALITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any schema that forces all transport-supported path composites between the same endpoints to be indistinguishable whenever a witnessed obstruction exist

entries.label: ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any schema that forces Met-consistency to imply commutation of transport composition in cases where order-sensitivity has been witnessed.

entries.label: NO_EQUALITY_FROM_METRIC
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may assert that Met(m,n,v) implies identity or equality of m and n, nor introduce substitution power from Met.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_TRIANGLE_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any universal schema asserting a triangle-like law over value tokens.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_SYMMETRY_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any universal schema asserting Met(m,n,v) implies Met(n,m,v) for all m,n,v.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_REFLEXIVITY_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any universal schema asserting existence of v such that Met(m,m,v) for all m.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_SEPARATION_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may include any schema asserting that distinctness is implied by non-canonical values, or that canonical values imply indistinguishability.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_COMPATIBLE_METRIC_SUPPORT
entries.body: Any asserted Met(m,n,v) is admissible only if supported by CompM(m,n) and the existence of at least one path token π with End(π,m,n) supported by transport chains.

entries.label: GEOMETRY_COMPATIBLE_METRIC
entries.body: No admissible metric-like structure may assert Met instances on token pairs lacking any geometry-layer support relation that connects those tokens via compatibility or incidence.

entries.label: ORDERED_VALUE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of introducing an order relation on value tokens is open, provided it does not introduce completed infinities, numeric primitives, or optimization semantics.

entries.label: COORDINATE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of coordinate-like representations derived from Met is open, provided they are removable overlays and introduce no substitution power.

entries.label: DIMENSION_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of derived dimensionality notions from Met is open, provided no coordinate or rank primitives are introduced.

entries.label: NORMED_SPACE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of norm-like structures is open, provided they are admitted as relations over finite value-token registries without numeric primitives.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes frozen carrier M, compatibility CompM, path tokens with End and Cat, transport Tr/TrC, obstruction Obs/ObsM, and any admitted metric-like relations Met/MetP.

entries.label: COORDINATE_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: A coordinate-like representation is admissible only as an explicitly declared relation Coord(x,c) where x is a carrier token or path token and c is a token in an explicitly declared finite coordinate-

entries.label: COORDINATE_MAP_PARTIALITY
entries.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assume Coord(x,c) exists for all x, nor uniqueness of c for any x.

entries.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_COORDINATES
entries.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assert that sharing a coordinate token implies identity, equality, or indistinguishability of carrier or path tokens.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_COORDINATES
entries.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may introduce any schema permitting replacement of one token by another on the basis of Coord.

entries.label: REPRESENTATION_ONLY
entries.body: Any coordinate-like relation Coord is admissible only as a removable representation layer; no admissible specification may treat Coord as constraining or redefining carrier, transport, obstruction, ge

entries.label: NO_CANONICAL_COORDINATIZATION
entries.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assume a canonical or privileged coordinate assignment for any token or relation.

entries.label: COMPATIBILITY_SUPPORT
entries.body: Any asserted Coord(x,c) is admissible only if supported by at least one frozen relation instance involving x (e.g., CompM, End, Inc, Met), without introducing any new kernel constraint.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Coord assignments.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Coord assignments.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_COORDINATE_SYSTEM
entries.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assert the existence of a global coordinate system covering all carrier tokens or all compatible pairs.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_DIMENSION_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may assert any dimensionality, rank, or coordinate-length property of C or of Coord assignments.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CHART_ATLAS_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible coordinate-like representation may introduce chart or atlas structures with overlap rules unless explicitly declared OPEN in a later extension.

entries.label: CHART_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of chart-like collections of coordinate relations is open, provided no dimensionality, rank, or metric primitives are introduced and Coord remains removable.

entries.label: ATLAS_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of atlas-like structures organizing multiple chart-like collections is open, provided Coord remains non-authoritative and introduces no substitution power.

entries.label: DIMENSIONALITY_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of derived dimensionality notions from purely relational properties of coordinate-token registries is open, provided no rank primitives or completed infinities are introduced.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes frozen carrier M with compatibility CompM, transport Tr/TrC, obstruction Obs/ObsM, geometry relations, and optional removable coordinate relations Coord.

entries.label: DIMENSION_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: A dimensionality-like claim is admissible only as an explicitly declared relation Dim(A,d) where A is a token denoting a finite declared family of relation instances or representation instances, and d

entries.label: DIMENSION_TOKEN_NONNUMERIC
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may treat dimension tokens d as numbers, ordered quantities, or elements of a completed infinite domain.

entries.label: NO_PRIMITIVE_RANK
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may introduce rank primitives, basis primitives, or coordinate-length primitives as part of Dim.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_DIMENSIONALITY
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assume that Dim(A,d) is defined for all admissible families A, nor that any canonical d exists for any A.

entries.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_DIM
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assert that equality of dimension tokens implies identity, equality, or substitution among carrier tokens, paths, or relations.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_DIM
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any token or relation instance on the basis of Dim.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may include any schema forcing transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Dim declarations.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may include any schema forcing obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Dim declarations.

entries.label: PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assume dimensionality claims exist for all compatibility components of M.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_AXIS_SYSTEM
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may introduce any axis system, axis basis, coordinate rank, or vector-space basis structure at this layer.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CANONICAL_DIMENSION
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assert that any family A has a unique canonical dimension token d independent of admissible representation choices.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_DIMENSION_ADDITIVITY_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible dimensionality-like structure may assert any universal additivity or product-like rule for Dim over composition of families A.

entries.label: AXIS_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of axis-system notions derived from Dim is open, provided they are removable representations and introduce no substitution power.

entries.label: COORDINATE_RANK_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of coordinate-rank notions is open, provided rank is not treated as primitive and dimensionality remains nonnumeric and non-authoritative.

entries.label: VECTOR_SPACE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of vector-space-like structures is open, provided they are introduced without assuming completed infinities and without binding kernel admissibility.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes frozen carrier M, state tokens, compatibility CompM, transport Tr/TrC, obstruction Obs/ObsM, and optional value-token registries used by prior metric and dimensionality

entries.label: FUNCTION_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: A function-like assignment is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation F(x,v) where x is a carrier token or state token and v is a token in an explicitly declared finite value-token r

entries.label: FUNCTION_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assume F(x,v) exists for all x, nor uniqueness of v for any x.

entries.label: NONAXIS_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assert that F induces an axis, coordinate system, basis, parameterization, or ordering of the carrier.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_PARAMETERIZATION_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible function-like assignment may include any universal schema asserting that carrier tokens are exhaustively represented or enumerated via F-values.

entries.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_FUNCTION_VALUES
entries.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assert that shared function values imply identity, equality, or indistinguishability of tokens.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_FUNCTION_VALUES
entries.body: No admissible function-like assignment may introduce any schema permitting replacement of one token by another on the basis of F.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_COMPATIBILITY
entries.body: Any assertion of F(x,v) that is used jointly with transport must not include any schema forcing transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to F-

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_COMPATIBILITY
entries.body: Any assertion of F(x,v) that is used jointly with obstruction must not include any schema forcing obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to F-values.

entries.label: NO_TOTAL_FUNCTION_BY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assert existence of a total function over all carrier tokens or all state tokens.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_ORDER_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible function-like assignment may introduce any order relation on V and then assert a universal induced order on carrier or state tokens from F.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_ADDITIVITY_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible function-like assignment may assert any universal additivity, linearity, or homomorphism-like schema for combining F-values across transport composition or path concatenation.

entries.label: AXIS_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of promoting certain families of function-like assignments into true axes or coordinate systems is open, provided this introduces no substitution power and remains removable.

entries.label: OPTIMIZATION_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of using function values in optimization-like selection is open, provided time, probability, and utility semantics are not introduced as primitives.

entries.label: ORDERED_VALUE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of introducing order on value tokens is open, provided it does not induce global ordering or parameterization of the carrier by default.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes frozen axis-like function admissibility for individual function relations F_i(x,v), and frozen carrier/transport/obstruction structure.

entries.label: AXIS_SET_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: A finite axis-set is admissible only as an explicitly declared token Σ together with a finite declared membership relation Mem(Σ,F) where each member F is an admitted axis-like function relation symbo

entries.label: AXIS_SET_SCOPE_RESTRICTION
entries.body: No admissible axis-set Σ may contain any symbol that is not an admitted axis-like function relation symbol.

entries.label: NONCOORDINATE_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may assert that Σ determines a coordinate system, frame, basis, or global parameterization of carrier tokens or state tokens.

entries.label: NO_RANK_OR_COMPLETENESS
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may assert any rank, completeness, spanning, or exhaustiveness property of Σ relative to the carrier or any family of relations.

entries.label: NO_INDEPENDENCE_ASSUMPTION
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may assume linear independence, orthogonality, functional independence, or non-redundancy among its members.

entries.label: NO_CLOSURE_OR_SPAN
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may assert closure of Σ under any combination rule, nor assert that Σ generates or spans any larger class of functions.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to joint use of members of Σ.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to joint use of members of Σ.

entries.label: PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may assume all its member functions are defined for all carrier tokens or all state tokens.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_AXIS_SET
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may assume existence of a single axis-set Σ that applies globally to all compatibility components or all declared admissible domains.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_INDUCED_ORDER
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may introduce any schema that induces a global order on carrier tokens or state tokens by aggregating values across members of Σ.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_VECTOR_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible axis-set may assert that tuples of values across members of Σ form vectors, coordinates, or elements of any structured product space with substitution power.

entries.label: COORDINATE_FRAME_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of promoting certain axis-sets into coordinate frames or bases is open, provided this remains removable and introduces no substitution power.

entries.label: ORTHOGONALITY_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of orthogonality-like or independence-like relations among members of Σ is open, provided no metric, rank, or completeness primitives are introduced.

entries.label: COMPLETENESS_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of completeness or spanning-like notions for Σ is open, provided no global parameterization or coordinate semantics is asserted by default.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes admitted axis-like function symbols F,G,… and admitted finite axis-sets Σ with membership Mem(Σ,F), under frozen carrier/transport/obstruction structure.

entries.label: ORTHOGONALITY_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: An orthogonality-like relation is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Orth(F,G) ranging over admitted axis-like function relation symbols.

entries.label: ORTH_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assume Orth(F,G) is defined for all pairs of function symbols, nor assume uniqueness or completeness of Orth structure.

entries.label: NONMETRIC_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assert or imply any metric, norm, distance, or inner product structure on carrier tokens, state tokens, path tokens, or value-token registries.

entries.label: NOBASIS_NORANK_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assert or imply bases, coordinate frames, rank, completeness, dimensionality, or spanning properties for any axis-set or family of functions.

entries.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_ORTH
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assert that Orth(F,G) implies identity, equality, or substitutability of function symbols or any tokens they reference.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_ORTH
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may introduce any schema permitting replacement of tokens or function symbols on the basis of Orth.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Orth declarations.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Orth declarations.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_SYMMETRY_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any universal schema asserting Orth(F,G) implies Orth(G,F) for all F,G.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_REFLEXIVITY_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any universal schema asserting Orth(F,F) for all F.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_TRANSITIVITY_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may include any universal schema asserting Orth(F,G) and Orth(G,H) implies Orth(F,H).

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_GLOBAL_ORTHOGONAL_SET
entries.body: No admissible orthogonality-like relation may assert existence of a global orthogonal family or global orthogonalization procedure over all admitted functions.

entries.label: INNER_PRODUCT_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of inner-product-like structures is open, provided they are introduced without assuming metrics, bases, rank, or dimensionality as primitives.

entries.label: NORMED_SPACE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of normed-space-like structure is open, provided it does not introduce completed infinities and does not bind kernel admissibility.

entries.label: HILBERT_LIKE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of Hilbert-like structures is open, provided they remain removable overlays and introduce no substitution power.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes admitted finite axis-sets Σ with membership Mem(Σ,F), admitted axis-like functions, and frozen carrier/transport/obstruction structure.

entries.label: COMPLETENESS_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: A completeness- or spanning-like claim is admissible only as an explicitly declared unary relation symbol Comp(Σ) ranging over admitted axis-set tokens Σ.

entries.label: COMPLETENESS_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assume Comp(Σ) holds for all admitted axis-sets, nor that Comp(Σ) is decidable or witnessed for any Σ by default.

entries.label: NONBASIS_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assert or imply that any Σ satisfying Comp forms a basis, coordinate frame, or coordinate system for carrier or state tokens.

entries.label: NORANK_NODIMENSION_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assert or imply rank, dimensionality, minimal cardinality, or uniqueness of any Σ satisfying Comp.

entries.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_COMPLETENESS
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assert that Comp(Σ) implies identity, equality, or substitutability of any carrier tokens, state tokens, or function symbols.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_COMPLETENESS
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any token or function symbol on the basis of Comp.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Comp declarations.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to Comp declarations.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_COMPLETENESS
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may assert existence of a global canonical axis-set Σ* such that Comp(Σ*) and Σ* applies across all compatibility components or admissible domains.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_SPAN_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any universal schema asserting that Comp(Σ) implies every admissible axis-like function is generated, derived, or representable by members of Σ.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_UNIQUENESS_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any universal schema asserting uniqueness or equivalence of any two axis-sets satisfying Comp.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_ORTHOGONAL_COMPLETENESS_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible completeness-like claim may include any schema asserting that Orth relations plus Comp imply basis-like structure, orthonormality, or coordinate representation.

entries.label: BASIS_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of promoting certain Comp(Σ) claims into basis or frame notions is open, provided these remain removable overlays and introduce no substitution power.

entries.label: SPANNING_SYSTEM_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of spanning-like systems that introduce representability constraints is open, provided no rank, dimensionality, or global coordinate semantics is asserted by default.

entries.label: HILBERT_COMPLETENESS_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of Hilbert-style completeness notions is open, provided they do not bind kernel admissibility and do not assume completed infinities.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes admitted composition symbols for transport composition (when defined), admitted transport instances, and admitted witnessing of order-sensitivity and obstruction under f

entries.label: CLASSCOMP_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: A composition-classification is admissible only as an explicitly declared classifier relation ClassComp(CompSym,k) where CompSym ranges over explicitly declared composition symbols and k ranges over a

entries.label: CLASSIFICATION_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assume ClassComp(CompSym,k) is defined for all composition symbols, nor that each CompSym belongs to exactly one class token in K.

entries.label: NONALGEBRA_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert or imply any group, ring, field, vector-space, module, or algebraic structure over composition symbols, tokens, or relations.

entries.label: NO_CLOSURE_BY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert closure of composition within any class or across classes, nor assert that composing two admissible compositions yields an admissible composition sy

entries.label: NO_TOTALITY_BY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert that composition is total on any declared domain of symbols or instances.

entries.label: NO_ASSOCIATIVITY_BY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert associativity of composition symbols or composition instances unless explicitly placed in OPEN.

entries.label: NO_IDENTITY_ELEMENT_BY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert existence of an identity composition symbol or neutral element unless explicitly placed in OPEN.

entries.label: NO_INVERTIBILITY_BY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert invertibility of composition symbols or instances unless explicitly placed in OPEN.

entries.label: ORDER_SENSITIVITY_CLASS_DISTINCTIONS
entries.body: Composition classes may be used to distinguish admissible order-sensitivity witnesses, and no admissible schema may force commutation of compositions by default.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to ClassComp declarations

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to ClassComp declarations.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_CLASS_PARTITION
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert a single global partition of all admissible composition symbols into classes by default.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_EXHAUSTIVENESS_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert that for every composition symbol CompSym there exists k in K with ClassComp(CompSym,k).

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_UNIQUENESS_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible composition-classification may assert that for every composition symbol CompSym there exists exactly one k in K with ClassComp(CompSym,k).

entries.label: ASSOCIATIVITY_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of associativity for restricted composition families is open, provided it introduces no algebraic primitives and does not trivialize order-sensitivity witnesses.

entries.label: IDENTITY_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of identity-like composition symbols for restricted families is open, provided no substitution power or global totality is introduced.

entries.label: ALGEBRAIC_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of algebraic structures over composition symbols is open, provided it remains removable and does not bind kernel admissibility.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes frozen carrier/state token admission, transport and obstruction instances, and optional axis-function and axis-set structures, without assuming any total classification

entries.label: DUALITY_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: A duality-like structure is admissible only as an explicitly declared binary relation symbol Dual(x,y) or an explicitly declared classifier relation Class2(x,c) where x ranges over admitted carrier to

entries.label: DUALITY_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assume Dual(x,y) or Class2(x,c) is defined for all admissible x, nor that every x belongs to exactly one class token in C2.

entries.label: NONONTOLOGY_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert or imply two kinds of being, substance, or necessity; duality is classification of admitted tokens only.

entries.label: NONAXIS_NONCOORDINATE_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert or imply axes, coordinate frames, bases, rank, dimensionality, or any global parameterization of the carrier.

entries.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_DUALITY
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert that duality or class membership implies identity, equality, or indistinguishability of tokens or relation instances.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_DUALITY
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any token or relation instance on the basis of Dual or Class2 membership.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to duality declarations.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to duality declarations.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_PARTITION_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may include any universal schema asserting a mandatory two-class partition of all admissible objects in any declared domain.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CLASS_EXHAUSTIVENESS_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert that for every x there exists c in C2 such that Class2(x,c).

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CLASS_UNIQUENESS_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert that for every x there exists exactly one c in C2 such that Class2(x,c).

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_INVOLUTION_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible duality-like structure may assert existence of a universal involution map Inv(x) or equivalence-like pairing schema derived from Dual by default.

entries.label: INVOLUTION_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of involution-like maps is open, provided they introduce no identity, equality, substitution power, or global totality by default.

entries.label: CONJUGATION_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of conjugation-like pairings is open, provided they remain structural classifications and do not induce coordinate or metric structure.

entries.label: SYMMETRY_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of symmetry-like notions relating duality classes is open, provided no ontological necessity or global partition is asserted.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes admitted path tokens π formed from admissible transport compositions (when defined), admitted composition symbols, and preserved witnessing of order-sensitivity and obst

entries.label: CLASSCYCLE_SYMBOL_SCHEMA
entries.body: A cycle-classification is admissible only as an explicitly declared classifier relation ClassCycle(π,k) where π ranges over admitted path tokens and k ranges over a finite declared cycle-class token r

entries.label: CYCLE_PARTIALITY_DEFAULT
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assume ClassCycle(π,k) is defined for all path tokens, nor that each π belongs to exactly one class token in KC.

entries.label: NON_TEMPORAL_NON_DYNAMIC_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert or imply temporal recurrence, dynamics, evolution, rates, or causal iteration; cycle is a structural classification of paths only.

entries.label: NON_ENGINE_GUARANTEE
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert or imply engines, work, extraction, advantage, or optimization; cycle is not a machine schema.

entries.label: NO_IDENTITY_FROM_CYCLE
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert that ClassCycle(π,k) implies identity, equality, equivalence, or indistinguishability of paths, carrier tokens, or state tokens.

entries.label: NO_SUBSTITUTION_FROM_CYCLE
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may introduce any schema permitting replacement of any path or token on the basis of ClassCycle membership.

entries.label: TRANSPORT_ORDER_SENSITIVITY_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may include any schema that forces transport composition order-sensitivity witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to ClassCycle declarations.

entries.label: OBSTRUCTION_PRESERVATION
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may include any schema that forces obstruction witnesses to vanish or become indistinguishable solely due to ClassCycle declarations.

entries.label: NO_GLOBAL_CYCLE_PARTITION
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert a mandatory global partition of all admissible paths into cycle classes by default.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_EXHAUSTIVENESS_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert that for every admitted path token π there exists k in KC such that ClassCycle(π,k).

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_UNIQUENESS_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert that for every admitted path token π there exists exactly one k in KC such that ClassCycle(π,k).

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CLOSURE_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert closure of cycle-classes under path concatenation, nor assert that concatenation of two paths in the same class remains in that class, unless explicitly p

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CANONICAL_REPRESENTATIVE_SCHEMA
entries.body: No admissible cycle-classification may assert that each class k has a canonical representative path πk or canonical normal form by default.

entries.label: RECURRENCE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of recurrence-like interpretations is open, provided they do not introduce time, probability, causality, or optimization as primitives.

entries.label: DYNAMICS_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of dynamics-like structure on paths is open, provided it remains removable and does not bind kernel admissibility.

entries.label: ENGINE_EXTENSIONS
entries.body: Admissibility of engine-like interpretations of cycle classes is open, provided they remain non-binding overlays and introduce no optimization or extraction semantics into the kernel.

entries.label: DEPENDENCY
entries.body: This specification presupposes all frozen admissibility layers for carrier/state/transport/obstruction, composition-classes, and cycle-classes, and presupposes that no time-like, probability-like, cau

entries.label: BOUNDARY_STATEMENT
entries.body: No dynamics-like notion (including evolution, flow, rate, recurrence, update, or temporal iteration) is admissible as kernel structure under the frozen layers without admitting additional primitives b

entries.label: TIME_LIKE_PRIMITIVE_REQUIREMENT
entries.body: A time-like primitive capable of supporting ordered progression is open as a prerequisite for admitting dynamics beyond overlay status.

entries.label: PROBABILITY_LIKE_PRIMITIVE_REQUIREMENT
entries.body: A probability-like primitive capable of supporting stochastic update semantics is open as a prerequisite for admitting dynamics beyond overlay status.

entries.label: CAUSALITY_LIKE_PRIMITIVE_REQUIREMENT
entries.body: A causality-like primitive capable of supporting directed dependence claims is open as a prerequisite for admitting dynamics beyond overlay status.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_DYNAMICS_CLAIMS
entries.body: Any kernel claim asserting existence of a global update rule, step-indexed evolution, time-parameterized family of state maps, rate law, recurrence law, or convergent iteration is forbidden under the

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_TEMPORAL_SEMANTICS
entries.body: Any kernel claim asserting “before/after,” “next/previous,” temporal ordering of states, or temporal interpretation of paths is forbidden under the frozen layers.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_STOCHASTIC_SEMANTICS
entries.body: Any kernel claim asserting stochastic transitions, sampling, random evolution, or probabilistic trajectories is forbidden under the frozen layers.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_CAUSAL_SEMANTICS
entries.body: Any kernel claim asserting causal forcing, directed causal arrows, or causal explanation for refinement, transport, obstruction, composition-class, or cycle-class behavior is forbidden under the froze

entries.label: NON_BACKFLOW_GUARANTEE
entries.body: Any future admission of dynamics-like primitives or dynamics-like structures must not retroactively alter admissibility of any earlier layer, and must not reclassify previously forbidden structures as

entries.label: SAFE_OVERLAY_BOUNDARY
entries.body: Dynamics-like notions may appear only as removable, non-binding overlays that do not constrain or modify kernel admissibility, and any such overlay must remain deletable without changing any kernel-de

entries.label: FUTURE_DYNAMICS_ADMISSIBILITY_CONDITIONS

entries.label: FROZEN_STACK_AUTHORITY
entries.body: All named frozen admissibility specifications are authoritative and are not modified by any candidate proposal document.

entries.label: CANDIDATE_DOCUMENT_FORM
entries.body: A candidate proposal is a finite plain-text document consisting only of (i) a candidate header token, (ii) declarations, (iii) witness declarations, (iv) compatibility claims, (v) failure conditions,

entries.label: CANDIDATE_HEADER_TOKEN
entries.body: The first non-empty line of a candidate proposal document MUST be a single fixed token string naming the candidate; this token string is the candidate’s identity and must be treated as uninterpreted.

entries.label: NO_RENAMING_RULE
entries.body: A candidate proposal MUST NOT rename, paraphrase, alias, or reinterpret its own header token, and MUST NOT rename, paraphrase, alias, or reinterpret any frozen specification name or frozen symbol name

entries.label: VERSIONING_AS_TOKEN_ONLY
entries.body: Any versioning of a candidate identity MUST be encoded only in the candidate header token string; no semantic meaning of version increments is admitted at the kernel level.

entries.label: DECLARATION_BLOCK_REQUIRED
entries.body: A candidate proposal MUST contain a declaration block that enumerates exactly the set of new symbols it proposes and exactly the set of frozen symbols it uses.

entries.label: NEW_SYMBOL_DISCIPLINE
entries.body: New symbol declarations MUST be limited to (i) finite token registries, (ii) relation symbols over previously admitted token types, and (iii) classifier symbols over previously admitted token types; n

entries.label: FROZEN_SYMBOL_USAGE_LIST
entries.body: A candidate proposal MUST list all frozen symbols it invokes; omission of an invoked frozen symbol from the usage list is a failure condition.

entries.label: WITNESS_BLOCK_REQUIRED
entries.body: A candidate proposal MUST contain a witness block whenever it makes any claim about non-commutation, obstruction, nontriviality, order-sensitivity, non-flattenability, incompatibility, or any other cl

entries.label: WITNESS_SYNTAX_ONLY
entries.body: A witness is admissible only as a finite syntactic binding between declared symbols/tokens; witnesses MUST NOT be treated as semantic, evidentiary, evaluative, probabilistic, temporal, or causal objec

entries.label: WITNESS_TYPING
entries.body: Each witness declaration MUST specify the witness token name and the finite tuple of declared tokens/symbols it binds; witness typing MUST be explicit and must not assume identity or equality.

entries.label: COMPATIBILITY_CLAIM_BLOCK_REQUIRED
entries.body: A candidate proposal MUST contain a compatibility claim block that lists which frozen specifications it claims to satisfy.

entries.label: CLAIM_FORM
entries.body: Each compatibility claim MUST be stated as a finite declarative statement referencing only declared symbols and/or listed witnesses; each claim MUST be written so that its well-formedness can be check

entries.label: NO_IMPLIED_ADMISSIBILITY
entries.body: A candidate proposal MUST NOT assert that any structure is admissible “by default,” “implicitly,” or “because it is standard”; admissibility must be claimed only via explicit compatibility claims agai

entries.label: FAILURE_CONDITION_BLOCK_REQUIRED
entries.body: A candidate proposal MUST contain an explicit failure condition block enumerating falsifiers as syntactic conditions (e.g., undefined symbol use, forbidden symbol introduction, missing witness where r

entries.label: NO_PATCHING_RULE
entries.body: A candidate proposal MUST declare that if any listed failure condition holds, the candidate is eliminated as a candidate; no patching, repair, reinterpretation, or partial acceptance is permitted with

entries.label: NON_BACKFLOW_GUARANTEE
entries.body: Candidate elimination MUST NOT modify, weaken, override, or retroactively reinterpret any frozen admissibility layer; candidate failure affects only the candidate’s status, not the frozen stack.

entries.label: FORBIDDEN_PRIMITIVE_IMPORTS
entries.body: A candidate proposal MUST NOT introduce as admitted primitives any time-like, probability-like, causality-like, optimization-like, ontological, or teleological structure; if referenced, such items mus

entries.label: CANDIDATE_FAMILY_LISTING
entries.body: A candidate proposal MAY include an OPEN-only list of candidate-family header tokens as non-binding placeholders; listing a family does not assert existence, preference, correctness, or admissibility.

entries.label: FUTURE_FORMAT_REFINEMENTS
entries.body: Additional format constraints (e.g., canonical ordering of blocks, stricter typing grammars, stronger witness schemas) are OPEN provided they do not introduce new admitted primitives and do not alter
